title: BUbreakingSectionTwo
description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from BUbreakingSectionTwo, skipping any feeds with errors.

items.title: Au Pair Juliana Peres Magalhães Sentenced to 10 Years in Banfield Double Murder Case

items.title: Analog-obsessed Gen Zers are buying $40 app blockers to limit their social media use and take a break from the ‘slot machine in your pocket’
items.description: App-blocking devices like Bloom and Brick promise to improve focus and decrease compulsive phone checks by making social media harder to access.

items.title: Venezuela’s Natural Gas, Not Oil, Might Be a Big Early Prize

items.title: San Francisco Teachers End Strike After 4 Days
items.description: Public schools are expected to reopen on Wednesday for 50,000 students in the city. Teachers demanded higher wages and health care benefits.

items.title: Mamdani Revives Bus and Bike Lanes Killed by Adams in ‘Backroom Deals’

items.title: New York Fed economists confirm that Americans are the ones footing the bill for 90% of Trump’s tariffs
items.description: The president has insisted other countries are paying for his import taxes. American companies and consumers are telling a different story.

items.title: Exclusive: Binance fires top investigators who claim to have uncovered evidence of Iranian sanctions violations
items.description: Internal findings by the fired investigators reviewed by Fortune pointed to over $1 billion in Tether flowing through Binance to Iran-linked wallets.

items.title: Exclusive:  The Walton family-funded PE firm that owns Rapha Cycling Club presses pause on all new investments
items.description: RZC Investments, the private equity firm of Walmart heirs Tom and Steuart Walton, said it was still “actively” managing its current portfolio.

items.title: Top analyst warns the economy is figuring out how to grow without creating new jobs, leaving a major vulnerability

items.title: Japan Seizes Chinese Fishing Boat; Move Likely to Add to Tensions
items.description: The seizure of the trawler, which Japan said was sailing in its waters in the East China Sea, is likely to add to tensions between Tokyo and Beijing.

items.title: Can’t Buy Love? Kenya Bans Bouquets Made of Cash.
items.description: Floral arrangements crafted from carefully-folded, colorful bank notes, had become a popular symbol of love in Nairobi.
